codeReview.summary: Commit 0bd1bd2 adds the six requested command TOML files and the files do satisfy the core product requirements: there are now nine command files total, each new file is valid TOML with description/prompt fields, mission-plan and mission-run reference the expected skills, pause/resume mention the required state transitions, export uses tar.gz, and override records justification. However, the worker's execution record is not fully trustworthy because it claims the extension-builder skill was unavailable even though the project-local skill file exists and documents this exact work.

issues.description: The handoff says the worker could not invoke the extension-builder skill because it was not available, but the repository already contains /Users/codex/Projects/gemini-mission-control-extension/.factory/skills/extension-builder/SKILL.md. The feature output is correct, but this makes the recorded deviation inaccurate.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Workers are still reporting project-local extension-builder as unavailable even though the skill file exists in .factory/skills. Either worker instructions for locating project-local skills are unclear, or the runtime does not expose those skills consistently.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The project contains /Users/codex/Projects/gemini-mission-control-extension/.factory/skills/extension-builder/SKILL.md, while the reviewed handoff at lines 71-77 says the skill was not found and recommends registering/installing it before worker dispatch.

summary: Reviewed the transcript skeleton, handoff, commit diff, command files, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md, services.yaml, and the local extension-builder skill. The six TOML files themselves satisfy the feature requirements, but I marked the review failed because the execution record contains a material inaccuracy about skill availability, which lowers confidence in the worker's reported procedure even though the shipped files are correct.
